title: What Drives Interactive Improvement from Feedback?
author: Bart{\l}omiej Cupia{\l}, Jan {\L}ojek, Miko{\l}aj Garstecki, Szymon Pob{\l}ocki, Alicja Ziarko, Piotr Mi{\l}o\'s
raw_description: arXiv:2606.30774v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We study when natural-language feedback produces improvement beyond the gains obtainable from repeated attempts alone. In multi-turn language agent setting, higher final accuracy can reflect useful feedback, but it can also arise from resampling, format correction, or additional test-time computation. To separate these effects, we introduce a controlled student-teacher protocol across Omni-MATH, Codeforces, BBEH Linguini, and ARC-AGI1, evaluating thirteen open-weight models in both student and teacher roles. We compare external feedback, self-feedback, and unguided self-refinement, while varying interaction history, task difficulty, and teacher access to privileged task information. Across settings, we find that multi-turn improvement is often not evidence of feedback use: self-generated feedback adds little beyond unguided self-refinement, whereas the strongest external teachers produce substantially larger feedback-specific gains, sugge
summary: 该论文通过受控学生-教师协议，在多轮语言智能体设置中分离反馈带来的改进与重复尝试、格式修正等效应，发现自我反馈增益有限，而强外部教师能带来显著提升。
analysis_note: 实验设计严谨，揭示了反馈在LLM多轮交互中的真实作用，对理解自我改进机制有重要价值。
